report_name: CISA Mandates Federal Agencies Patch Actively Exploited Ivanti EPMM Flaw by April 11

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The vulnerability CVE-2026-1340 in Ivanti EPMM is being actively exploited in the wild, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by sending a specially crafted request to the server.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-1340
vulnerabilities.description: A critical code injection vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary commands. CISA has confirmed it is being actively exploited in the wild.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) Code Injection Vulnerability

identities.name: Federal Civilian Executive Branch
identities.description: A group of U.S. federal agencies mandated by CISA to patch the Ivanti EPMM vulnerability CVE-2026-1340 by April 11, 2026, due to active exploitation.

identities.name: Ivanti
identities.description: Vendor of the affected Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) product.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of Ivanti EPMM Service
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of Ivanti EPMM Service
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a1b-9c8d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects when the Ivanti EPMM application process (java or httpd) spawns a suspicious child process such as a command shell, which may indicate exploitation of CVE-2026-1340.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/08/cisa-adds-one-known-exploited-vulnerability-catalog
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-09
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects when the Ivanti EPMM application process (java or httpd) spawns a suspicious child process such as a command shell, which may indicate exploitation of CVE-2026-1340.

indicators.name: Execution of Suspicious Shells on Ivanti EPMM Server
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Execution of Suspicious Shells on Ivanti EPMM Server
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b9a-8f7e6d5c4b3a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common command shells on a system, which are noted as suspicious child processes to monitor for on potentially compromised Ivanti EPMM servers. This rule is broad and should be scoped to EPMM servers.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/08/cisa-adds-one-known-exploited-vulnerability-catalog
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-09
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common command shells, which are noted as suspicious child processes to monitor for on potentially compromised Ivanti EPMM servers.
